compliance:
Compliance:
Overall assessment of compliance management in the organisation. Systematic follow-up of findings from the Competent Authority, internal audits versus the compliance monitoring programme.
Is the organisation audited regularly by other external parties? (IOSA, sub-contractors etc.)
flightops:
Flight Operations:
Qualifications and staffing. Chief Pilot, support personnel. (senior pilots, technical pilots, bases etc.) Supervision, line check.
Recruitment, competence and qualification requirements.
Regulatory changes — are these handled properly?
Planning; new routes, categorisation of aerodromes, ground handling agreements etc.
safety:
Safety Department:
Sufficient resources? Assessment of the safety department. Safety objectives and policy. Realistic SPIs that are evaluated on an ongoing basis?
Is the effect of mitigating actions measured? Hazard and risk register.
Are causal factors analysed for occurrences with moderate and high risk? Are safety barriers for significant risk clearly defined?
Are operational or organisational changes subject to risk assessment or analysis beforehand?
Is SMS training for relevant personnel carried out and systematised?
Is a communication channel for safety-critical information established and effective?
training:
Training:
Staffing, NPCT and support functions.
Fixed wing: CC training.
Standardisation of instructors.
Training programme — does it give the desired outcome? Are line check and supervision included in the evaluation?
planning:
Planning & FTL:
Roster combinations, FRM and ongoing assessments. Fatigue reports.
reporting:
Reporting:
Availability for all personnel.
Just Culture principles. Safety culture.
Follow-up and feedback to the reporter.
other:
Other:
Brief overall assessment of the operator. If a 36-month cycle is to be considered or not, this shall be described here.
